steps.title: Capture the broad impact
steps.content: The passage describes several effects of mass-produced bicycles: people could travel farther, live farther from work, visit nearby towns, join leisure and sport activities, and form cycling clubs. The main idea must cover this range of new possibilities.

steps.title: Reject narrow summaries
steps.content: Choice B overstates affordability as making bicycles the preferred way to travel. Choice C focuses only on cycling clubs, one detail among many. Choice D introduces safety, which the passage never discusses. The answer is A.
